codes.title: Expected metadata boundary
codes.default_summary: Internal telemetry component was denied direct executable-data reads as expected.
codes.default_why: The component may classify package metadata but does not need direct file-data access to executable directories.
codes.default_next_action: No action required; this is a successful policy boundary.
codes.notes: Expected denials here should not be rendered as errors.

codes.title: Expected network disabled
codes.default_summary: Network access was blocked by policy as expected.
codes.default_why: The operation is required to use local-first verification or cached evidence unless policy explicitly allows network access.
codes.default_next_action: No action required unless the caller expected policy-authorized network access.
codes.notes: This models local-first safety, not a connectivity failure.

codes.title: Unexpected file read
codes.default_summary: A file read was denied because it was outside the actor's expected access profile.
codes.default_why: The operation did not match a known expected metadata, package, or repair boundary.
codes.default_next_action: Review actor provenance, target class, parent trace, and recent policy changes.
codes.notes: Use attack-like code if this crosses privilege, identity, agent, or kernel boundaries.

codes.title: Attack-like privilege boundary probe
codes.default_summary: A privilege boundary probe was blocked.
codes.default_why: The actor attempted an operation against a privileged identity, kernel, management, credential, or agent-control boundary outside its capability profile.
codes.default_next_action: Preserve forensic evidence, inspect parent trace, verify actor identity, and review recent agent or service launches.
codes.notes: This is not expected operator noise.

codes.title: Trust degraded to local-only
codes.default_summary: Trust evaluation degraded to local-only verification.
codes.default_why: Remote or enriched trust evidence was unavailable or disallowed, so SourceOS used local-first evidence and preserved the degradation state.
codes.default_next_action: Review local attestation status, package receipt, content hash, and policy governing remote trust lookup.
codes.notes: Degradation is explainable and bounded; it is not automatically compromise.

codes.title: Expected entitlement boundary
codes.default_summary: A management or identity lookup was blocked by an entitlement boundary.
codes.default_why: The responsible control plane refused access because the caller lacked the required entitlement or capability.
codes.default_next_action: No action required unless the probe correlates with an unexpected admin session, profile installation, or new privileged process.
codes.notes: Supports observed Apple MDM entitlement-denial fixtures while preserving SourceOS semantics.

codes.title: DarkWake network keepalive allowed
codes.default_summary: DarkWake network maintenance was allowed by policy.
codes.default_why: The system maintained network or TCP keep-alive state while the user-facing device appeared asleep.
codes.default_next_action: Record wake reason, beneficiary service, byte counts, duration, endpoint class when known, and policy authorization.
codes.notes: Power/wake receipts should be legible rather than buried in driver logs.

codes.title: Expected test boundary
codes.default_summary: Example expected policy boundary was enforced.
codes.default_why: This smoke test proves generated policy-decision events validate against the canonical schema.
codes.notes: Used by Makefile validation smoke test.
